title: AI Chat and Crypto Swap Privacy: What Not to Paste Before You Exchange
keyQuestions: Is it safe to ask an AI chat about a crypto swap?
What should users avoid pasting into an AI chat before a crypto swap?
Why can wallet addresses and TXIDs become sensitive in AI prompts?
How can screenshots expose crypto swap privacy details?
How can users ask AI for swap help without exposing wallet activity?
When should a crypto swap issue move from AI chat to verified support?
quickSteps: Ask the AI chat for general checks, not private route inspection.
Replace wallet addresses, TXIDs, exact amounts, and screenshots with neutral placeholders.
Keep live quote, network, amount, and destination checks on the current route page.
Share only narrow public facts with verified support if a real issue appears.
Stop immediately if any tool or person asks for seed phrases, private keys, backups, or remote access.

issueRouting.start: User wants to ask an AI chat for crypto swap help without exposing private wallet, route, or identity details.

branches.if: The question is general and does not require exact route inspection
then: Ask for a checklist, definition, or risk explanation.
Use neutral placeholders instead of wallet addresses, TXIDs, and exact amounts.
Keep the live route decision on the current swap page and wallet prompt.

branches.if: The user wants to paste screenshots, support messages, or explorer links
then: Rewrite the issue as a short neutral summary.
Remove addresses, balances, handles, case IDs, timestamps, and unrelated transaction history.
Share the original material only with verified support when narrow proof is actually needed.

branches.if: Any tool, person, or message asks for seed phrases, private keys, backups, or remote access
then: Do not paste or upload wallet control material anywhere.
Use only public transaction facts and verified support channels for troubleshooting.

riskNotes: A no-KYC swap flow can still lose privacy if the user pastes wallet addresses, TXIDs, screenshots, and route details into an AI chat.
Wallet addresses are public on-chain, but pasting them into prompts can connect them to writing style, route intent, timing, and other context.
TXIDs and explorer links can reveal chain, amount, fee, timing, source, destination, and surrounding wallet activity.
Screenshots can expose more than the swap box, including balances, tabs, notifications, browser details, support messages, and local context.
AI chats are useful for general checklists and explanations, but they should not replace the live route page, wallet prompt, or verified support.
